mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to downward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of existing homes and housing starts signals broad-based consumer confidence and credit availability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon through improved financial sector earnings and construction supply chains.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day S&P returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.05) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in cyclical equity sectors and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5-day S&P returns in over 55 percent of observations during early-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports consumer discretionary spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions show zero predictive power for 20-day S&P direction (Rank IC consistently near zero) across multiple macro cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 220, inflation expectations accelerate and force the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations to produce negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real existing home sales confirms synchronized demand across broad economic sectors, reducing recession probability and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling federal funds rates signals supply-side bottlenecks and policy misalignment, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The specified divergence pattern is followed by positive 60-day S&P returns in over 70 percent of historical occurrences.
